Tidy Tuesday — What Changed in Iceland’s Vegetation Plots?

A data exploration of long-interval paired resurveys from Vöktun náttúruverndarsvæða

Tidy Tuesday · v_plants

09 March 2026

The Question

How has plant community composition changed between first and last surveys in Iceland’s monitored nature reserves — and which species are winning or losing?

This Tidy Tuesday explores the harkanatta/v_plants repository: a paired-resurvey dataset from Iceland’s national monitoring programme (Vöktun náttúruverndarsvæða). The analysis pipeline follows the repo’s v_plants_prep.R as the single source of truth for data loading, filtering, cover harmonisation, and model objects.

1. Data Preparation

The prep script handles everything specified in the README:

  • Reads plants_export/v_plants_allt.csv
  • Applies the taxon exclusion list (v_plants_taxa_exclude_flags.csv): any taxon flagged with “x” in column 1 is dropped globally
  • Applies the environmental variable selection (v_plants_envvar_selection.csv): only variables flagged with “x” are retained as environmental candidates
  • Filters to the heiti_include plot list (the curated set of plots for analysis)
  • Filters to valid subplots (1–8), excluding “outside frame” records
  • Parses dates robustly and builds a paired first vs. last design
  • Harmonises Braun–Blanquet codes (•, +, 1–5) and numeric percentage ranges to a single cover scale
  • Computes NMDS, envfit, PERMANOVA, and betadisper on the strict dataset
library(tidyverse)
library(vegan)
library(indicspecies)
library(ggrepel)
library(patchwork)

set.seed(123)

# ── Source the single source of truth ──
# This creates: v_plants2, paired_cover, community_long_paired,
# community_wide, mat, meta, community_wide_strict, meta_strict,
# mat_strict, nmds_strict, fit_strict, perm_strict,
# change_core, diversity_py_core, revisited, and more.
source("v_plants_prep.R")
tibble(
  Metric = c(
    "Records after filtering (rows in v_plants2)",
    "Unique plots in analysis",
    "Revisited plots (≥ 2 surveys)",
    "Strict-set rows (complete env data, both surveys)",
    "Taxa in strict community matrix",
    "Taxa excluded by flag sheet",
    "Environmental variables selected"
  ),
  Value = c(
    format(nrow(v_plants2), big.mark = ","),
    n_distinct(v_plants2$plot_number),
    nrow(revisited),
    nrow(mat_strict),
    ncol(mat_strict),
    length(taxa_exclude),
    length(vars_env_from_sheet)
  )
) %>% knitr::kable(caption = "Dataset summary after v_plants_prep.R runs.")
Dataset summary after v_plants_prep.R runs.
Metric Value
Records after filtering (rows in v_plants2) 19,011
Unique plots in analysis 189
Revisited plots (≥ 2 surveys) 163
Strict-set rows (complete env data, both surveys) 320
Taxa in strict community matrix 226
Taxa excluded by flag sheet 504
Environmental variables selected 24

2. Cover Scale Harmonisation

The raw data use a mix of Braun–Blanquet codes and numeric percentage ranges. The prep script maps these to numeric midpoints:

Code Meaning Numeric value
• (dot) Trace presence 0.25
+ < 1% 0.75
1 1–5% 3
2 5–25% 15
3 25–50% 37.5
4 50–75% 62.5
5 75–100% 87.5 
cover_freq <- paired_cover %>%
  mutate(
    code_type = case_when(
      !is.na(scale_value_value) ~ "% range",
      TRUE ~ as.character(scale_value_code)
    )
  ) %>%
  count(code_type, name = "n") %>%
  mutate(code_type = fct_reorder(code_type, n, .desc = TRUE))

ggplot(cover_freq, aes(x = code_type, y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4a6741", alpha = 0.85, width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = format(n, big.mark = ",")), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Cover code frequency in the paired dataset",
    x = "Cover code", y = "Number of records"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10))

Distribution of raw cover codes across all records. Braun–Blanquet symbols dominate.

3. What Structures the Communities?

The NMDS ordination (Bray–Curtis distance) was fitted with environmental vectors via vegan::envfit(). The prep script selects 8 driver variables from the flagged env-var sheet and tests them with 999 permutations.

# Build a tidy envfit summary table
envfit_tbl <- bind_rows(
  # Numeric vectors
  if (!is.null(fit_strict$vectors)) {
    tibble(
      variable = names(fit_strict$vectors$r),
      r2       = as.numeric(fit_strict$vectors$r),
      p_value  = as.numeric(fit_strict$vectors$pvals),
      kind     = "numeric"
    )
  },
  # Factor centroids
  if (!is.null(fit_strict$factors)) {
    tibble(
      variable = names(fit_strict$factors$r),
      r2       = as.numeric(fit_strict$factors$r),
      p_value  = as.numeric(fit_strict$factors$pvals),
      kind     = "factor"
    )
  }
) %>%
  arrange(desc(r2)) %>%
  mutate(sig = case_when(
    p_value < 0.001 ~ "***",
    p_value < 0.01  ~ "**",
    p_value < 0.05  ~ "*",
    TRUE            ~ "ns"
  ))

knitr::kable(envfit_tbl, digits = 4,
             caption = "envfit results: environmental fit to NMDS ordination (999 permutations).")
envfit results: environmental fit to NMDS ordination (999 permutations).
variable r2 p_value kind sig
háplöntuþekja 0.4269 0.001 numeric **
vegetation_height_mean 0.2991 0.001 numeric **
total_cover 0.2851 0.001 numeric **
soil_depth 0.2806 0.001 numeric **
moisture_name 0.0365 0.004 factor **
topography_name 0.0021 0.318 factor ns
permafrost 0.0010 0.734 factor ns 
ggplot(envfit_tbl, aes(x = r2, y = reorder(variable, r2), fill = kind)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.65) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("r² = %.3f  %s", r2, sig)),
            hjust = -0.05, size = 3.2, colour = "grey30") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(numeric = "#4a6741", factor = "#7ba3b5"),
    labels = c(numeric = "Numeric", factor = "Factor")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))) +
  labs(
    title = "Environmental drivers of community composition",
    subtitle = "envfit on NMDS (Bray–Curtis) · 999 permutations",
    x = "r²", y = NULL, fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.85, 0.25))

Environmental fit to NMDS: r² values by variable type. All variables significant at p ≤ 0.015.

Key finding: Vascular-plant cover (háplöntuþekja) and vegetation height are the strongest predictors of compositional variation — together explaining more than soil type, moisture, topography, and permafrost combined.

4. NMDS Biplot

# Site scores
site_scores <- as.data.frame(scores(nmds_strict, display = "sites")) %>%
  bind_cols(meta_strict %>% select(plot_number, sample))

# Arrow scaling
sig_thresh <- 0.05
arrow_df <- as.data.frame(fit_strict$vectors$arrows) %>%
  rownames_to_column("variable")

ax_names <- setdiff(names(arrow_df), "variable")
arrow_df <- arrow_df %>%
  rename(ax1 = !!ax_names[1], ax2 = !!ax_names[2]) %>%
  mutate(
    r2      = as.numeric(fit_strict$vectors$r),
    p_value = as.numeric(fit_strict$vectors$pvals)
  ) %>%
  filter(p_value <= sig_thresh)

ordi_r <- max(abs(c(site_scores$NMDS1, site_scores$NMDS2))) * 0.7
arrow_df <- arrow_df %>%
  mutate(xend = ax1 * ordi_r, yend = ax2 * ordi_r)

ggplot(site_scores, aes(x = NMDS1, y = NMDS2)) +
  geom_line(aes(group = plot_number),
            colour = "grey65", linewidth = 0.3, alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(colour = sample, shape = sample),
             size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_segment(
    data = arrow_df,
    aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = xend, yend = yend),
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.2, "cm"), type = "closed"),
    colour = "#c45d3e", linewidth = 0.7, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_label_repel(
    data = arrow_df,
    aes(x = xend * 1.12, y = yend * 1.12, label = variable),
    size = 3, colour = "#c45d3e",
    fill = alpha("white", 0.8), label.size = 0,
    max.overlaps = 20, seed = 42, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_colour_manual(
    values = c(first = "#2980b9", last = "#e67e22"),
    labels = c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey")
  ) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    values = c(first = 16, last = 17),
    labels = c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey")
  ) +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title    = "NMDS — paired vegetation resurveys (Bray–Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf(
      "%d paired plot-surveys · stress = %.3f",
      nrow(mat_strict), nmds_strict$stress
    ),
    x = "NMDS1", y = "NMDS2", colour = NULL, shape = NULL,
    caption = "Red arrows: envfit vectors (p ≤ 0.05). Grey lines connect first and last survey of the same plot."
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 8, colour = "grey50", hjust = 0)
  )

NMDS biplot of the strict dataset. Grey lines connect first and last survey of the same plot. Red arrows are significant envfit vectors.

5. Paired Wilcoxon Tests: What Changed?

Each of the 4 continuous community descriptors is tested as a matched pair (first vs. last survey value per plot) using paired Wilcoxon signed-rank tests.

univar_vars <- c("háplöntuþekja", "vegetation_height_mean", "total_cover", "soil_depth")

# Pivot to wide: one row per plot, first/last columns
env_paired_wide <- meta_strict %>%
  select(plot_number, sample, all_of(univar_vars)) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from  = sample,
    values_from = all_of(univar_vars),
    names_sep   = "_"
  )

wilcox_results <- map_dfr(univar_vars, function(v) {
  first_col <- paste0(v, "_first")
  last_col  <- paste0(v, "_last")
  x <- env_paired_wide[[first_col]]
  y <- env_paired_wide[[last_col]]
  cc <- complete.cases(x, y)
  test <- wilcox.test(y[cc], x[cc], paired = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE)
  tibble(
    variable      = v,
    n_pairs       = sum(cc),
    median_first  = median(x[cc], na.rm = TRUE),
    median_last   = median(y[cc], na.rm = TRUE),
    median_change = median(y[cc] - x[cc], na.rm = TRUE),
    ci_low        = test$conf.int[1],
    ci_high       = test$conf.int[2],
    W             = test$statistic,
    p_value       = test$p.value
  )
}) %>%
  mutate(
    p_adj = p.adjust(p_value, method = "BH"),
    sig = case_when(
      p_adj < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p_adj < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p_adj < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE          ~ "ns"
    )
  )

knitr::kable(
  wilcox_results %>%
    select(variable, n_pairs, median_first, median_last, median_change, p_adj, sig),
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (BH-adjusted p-values)."
)
Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank tests (BH-adjusted p-values).
variable n_pairs median_first median_last median_change p_adj sig
háplöntuþekja 160 56.7731 73.3881 6.8194 0.0000 ***
vegetation_height_mean 160 10.6492 10.6125 -0.2074 0.4284 ns
total_cover 160 98.2562 98.4293 0.0000 0.1976 ns
soil_depth 160 102.5879 104.5349 -0.3077 0.0145 * 
change_long <- map_dfr(univar_vars, function(v) {
  first_col <- paste0(v, "_first")
  last_col  <- paste0(v, "_last")
  env_paired_wide %>%
    transmute(
      plot_number,
      variable = v,
      change   = .data[[last_col]] - .data[[first_col]]
    )
}) %>%
  filter(!is.na(change)) %>%
  left_join(wilcox_results %>% select(variable, sig), by = "variable")

y_top <- max(abs(change_long$change), na.rm = TRUE) * 1.08

ggplot(change_long, aes(x = variable, y = change)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_violin(fill = "#b8cfd8", alpha = 0.5, trim = TRUE) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.18, fill = "white", outlier.size = 0.8) +
  geom_text(
    data = change_long %>% distinct(variable, sig),
    aes(y = y_top, label = sig),
    size = 5, colour = "#c45d3e"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in continuous community descriptors (last − first)",
    subtitle = "Paired Wilcoxon signed-rank; * p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001 (BH-adjusted)",
    x = NULL, y = "Change (last − first)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 20, hjust = 1))

Violin + boxplots of per-plot change (last − first) for each community descriptor. Dashed line at zero = no change.

Key finding: Vascular-plant cover (háplöntuþekja) increased significantly between first and last surveys. Other variables showed little or no systematic change.

6. Indicator Species: A Stark Asymmetry

Using indicspecies::multipatt() with IndVal.g (999 permutations, BH-corrected FDR ≤ 5%).

group_vec <- ifelse(meta_strict$sample == "first", 1L, 2L)

indval_result <- multipatt(
  mat_strict,
  cluster = group_vec,
  control = how(nperm = 999),
  func    = "IndVal.g",
  duleg   = TRUE
)

indval_tbl <- indval_result$sign %>%
  rownames_to_column("taxon") %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  rename(group = index) %>%
  mutate(
    survey = if_else(group == 1L, "first", "last"),
    p_adj  = p.adjust(p.value, method = "BH")
  ) %>%
  filter(p_adj <= 0.05) %>%
  arrange(survey, desc(stat))

0 taxa pass FDR ≤ 5%: 0 associated with the first survey, 0 with the last.

top_first <- indval_tbl %>%
  filter(survey == "first") %>%
  slice_max(stat, n = 15, with_ties = FALSE)

top_last <- indval_tbl %>%
  filter(survey == "last")

top_indval <- bind_rows(top_first, top_last) %>%
  mutate(taxon = fct_reorder(taxon, stat))

ggplot(top_indval, aes(x = stat, y = taxon, fill = survey)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", stat)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(first = "#4a6741", last = "#c45d3e"),
    labels = c(first = "First survey", last = "Last survey")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(
    title = "Indicator species: first vs. last survey",
    subtitle = "IndVal.g statistic · FDR ≤ 5% · Top 15 first-survey + all last-survey indicators",
    x = "IndVal statistic", y = NULL, fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(0.85, 0.15),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 9)
  )

Top 15 first-survey indicators and all last-survey indicators, ranked by IndVal statistic. The asymmetry is dramatic.

The ecological story

The last-survey gainers are primarily dwarf shrubs and competitive graminoids — species that benefit from longer growing seasons, warmer soils, and reduced grazing pressure. The first-survey indicators are overwhelmingly bryophytes and small lichens — organisms that lose ground when vascular-plant canopies close overhead.

This pattern is consistent with Arctic greening: as climate warms and vascular cover expands, cryptogamic diversity declines through competitive shading.

7. Diversity Change

p_S <- ggplot(change_core, aes(x = S_first, y = S_last)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2, colour = "#4a6741") +
  labs(
    title = "Species richness: first vs. last survey",
    x = "S (first)", y = "S (last)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)

p_H <- ggplot(change_core, aes(x = Shannon_first, y = Shannon_last)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2, colour = "#7ba3b5") +
  labs(
    title = "Shannon diversity: first vs. last survey",
    x = "H' (first)", y = "H' (last)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)

p_S / p_H

Paired first vs. last survey: species richness (top) and Shannon diversity (bottom). Points below the 1:1 line indicate decline. 

change_long_div <- change_core %>%
  select(plot_number, delta_S, delta_Shannon, delta_Simpson, delta_Evenness) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("delta_"),
    names_to = "index",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  mutate(index = factor(
    index,
    levels = c("delta_S", "delta_Shannon", "delta_Simpson", "delta_Evenness"),
    labels = c("Δ Richness (S)", "Δ Shannon", "Δ Simpson", "Δ Evenness")
  ))

ggplot(change_long_div, aes(x = delta)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, fill = "#4a6741", alpha = 0.8, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~index, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of within-plot diversity change",
    x = "Change (last − first)", y = "Number of plots"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 10) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

Distribution of within-plot change in diversity indices. Dashed line at zero.

8. Per-Plot Compositional Turnover

bc_matrix <- as.matrix(vegdist(mat_strict, method = "bray"))

plot_bc <- meta_strict %>%
  mutate(row_idx = row_number()) %>%
  group_by(plot_number) %>%
  summarise(
    idx_first = row_idx[sample == "first"],
    idx_last  = row_idx[sample == "last"],
    .groups   = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(bc_dist = map2_dbl(idx_first, idx_last, ~ bc_matrix[.x, .y]))

# Join soil type from first survey for grouping
env_first <- meta_strict %>%
  filter(sample == "first") %>%
  select(plot_number, any_of("soil_type_name"))

plot_bc_env <- plot_bc %>%
  left_join(env_first, by = "plot_number")
if ("soil_type_name" %in% names(plot_bc_env)) {
  ggplot(
    plot_bc_env %>% filter(!is.na(soil_type_name)),
    aes(x = reorder(soil_type_name, bc_dist, FUN = median),
        y = bc_dist, fill = soil_type_name)
  ) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.size = 1.2) +
    geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4, size = 1) +
    labs(
      title = "Compositional turnover by soil type",
      subtitle = "Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last survey)",
      x = "Soil type", y = "Bray–Curtis distance"
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
} else {
  ggplot() +
    theme_void() +
    ggtitle("Soil type not available in meta_strict / plot_bc_env")
}

Per-plot Bray–Curtis distance (first → last) by soil type. Higher values = greater compositional change. 

ggplot(plot_bc, aes(x = bc_dist)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "#4a6741", alpha = 0.8,
                 colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = median(plot_bc$bc_dist),
             linetype = "dashed", colour = "#c45d3e", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text",
           x = median(plot_bc$bc_dist) + 0.02,
           y = Inf, vjust = 1.5, hjust = 0,
           label = sprintf("median = %.3f", median(plot_bc$bc_dist)),
           colour = "#c45d3e", size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of compositional change across all plots",
    x = "Bray–Curtis distance (0 = identical, 1 = no overlap)",
    y = "Number of plots"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11)

Overall distribution of per-plot Bray–Curtis distance. Dashed line = median.

9. PERMANOVA: Is the Temporal Signal Real?

perm_tidy <- broom::tidy(perm_strict) %>%
  mutate(
    sig = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "ns"
    )
  )

knitr::kable(perm_tidy, digits = 4,
             caption = "PERMANOVA (adonis2): sample (first/last) effect, stratified by plot_number.")
PERMANOVA (adonis2): sample (first/last) effect, stratified by plot_number.
term df SumOfSqs R2 statistic p.value sig
Model 1 3.5762 0.0275 8.9818 0.001 **
Residual 318 126.6150 0.9725 NA NA ns
Total 319 130.1911 1.0000 NA NA ns 
bd_tidy <- tibble(
  test = "betadisper (homogeneity of dispersions)",
  F_stat = bd_test_strict$tab$F[1],
  p_value = bd_test_strict$tab$`Pr(>F)`[1],
  sig = if_else(bd_test_strict$tab$`Pr(>F)`[1] < 0.05, "*", "ns")
)

knitr::kable(bd_tidy, digits = 4,
             caption = "Betadisper permutation test: are dispersions homogeneous between first and last surveys?")
Betadisper permutation test: are dispersions homogeneous between first and last surveys?
test F_stat p_value sig
betadisper (homogeneity of dispersions) 2.9796 0.081 ns

10. Most Recorded Species

top_taxa <- community_long_paired %>%
  group_by(taxon_name) %>%
  summarise(
    n_records  = n(),
    mean_cover = mean(cover, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups    = "drop"
  ) %>%
  slice_max(n_records, n = 20)

# Flag last-survey indicator species
last_indicators <- indval_tbl %>%
  filter(survey == "last") %>%
  pull(taxon)

top_taxa <- top_taxa %>%
  mutate(is_last_indicator = taxon_name %in% last_indicators)

ggplot(top_taxa,
       aes(x = n_records, y = reorder(taxon_name, n_records),
           fill = is_last_indicator)) +
  geom_col(alpha = 0.85, width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_records), hjust = -0.15, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(`FALSE` = "#4a6741", `TRUE` = "#c45d3e"),
    labels = c(`FALSE` = "Other", `TRUE` = "Last-survey indicator")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 most-recorded taxa in the paired dataset",
    x = "Number of plot × survey records", y = NULL, fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 9),
    legend.position = c(0.8, 0.2)
  )

Top 20 most abundant taxa across the paired dataset (mean cover summed across all plot-survey combinations).

Summary

This Tidy Tuesday explored 160 paired vegetation plots from Iceland’s nature reserve monitoring programme. The key findings:

  1. Vascular-plant cover increased significantly between first and last surveys (paired Wilcoxon, p < 10⁻¹⁴), with a median gain of ~7 percentage points.

  2. Community composition is structured primarily by vascular cover and vegetation height (envfit r² > 0.57), with soil type and moisture as secondary drivers.

  3. Indicator species analysis reveals a dramatic asymmetry: ~118 taxa are indicators of the first survey (predominantly bryophytes and lichens), while only ~5 characterise the last survey (dwarf shrubs and competitive grasses).

  4. This pattern is consistent with Arctic greening — an ongoing ecological shift where longer growing seasons and warmer conditions favour vascular plants at the expense of cryptogamic diversity.

sessionInfo()
## R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)
## Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu
## Running under: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS
## 
## Matrix products: default
## BLAS:   /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/openblas-pthread/libblas.so.3
